resources.string: ← Course Dashboard
Module
← Previous
Next Module →
Finish Course 🎉
Comprehensive Masterclass

Solving Cyber Crime
Using AI
Enter the Course
Course Curriculum
Course Modules
Master the tools of the future. Harness Large Language Models to detect anomalies, analyze malicious code, and accelerate incident response at machine speed.
Platform Coming Soon
Deep end-to-end modules covering everything from AI fundamentals to practical SOC weaponization.
Introduction to AI and Large Language Models
Fundamentals: Overview of AI, Machine Learning (ML), and Deep Learning (DL).
Generative AI & LLMs: How models process text, tokens, context windows, and transformers.
Prompt Engineering Basics: Best practices for communicating effectively with AI models.
Frontier AI Models & Custom Assistants
Latest Flagship Models: Mastering ChatGPT (GPT-5.4), Gemini (3.1 Pro), and Grok (Grok 4.20) for analysis.
NotebookLM: Uploading case files, FIRs, and logs to create instantly accessible audio overviews and AI investigations.
Custom GPTs & Gems: Building personalized, specialized AI agents pre-prompted for specific cyber defense tasks.
Deepfakes & AI-Assisted Detection
Understanding Deepfakes: How AI-generated face swaps and voice cloning are weaponized in cybercrime.
Visual Anomalies: Easy ways to spot artifacts like unnatural blinking, lighting inconsistencies, and blurred edges.
AI Detection Tools: Using accessible, non-technical AI tools to verify media authenticity and trace origins.
Mobile App (APK) Analysis
The Unzip Trick: How to rename an Android .apk file to .zip to extract its hidden contents without specialized software.
Analyzing the Code: Uploading extracted files (like AndroidManifest.xml or scripts) directly to Gemini for instant malware analysis.
Identifying Threats: Prompting AI to detect spyware, hidden APIs, tracking domains, and malicious permissions.
IndiaAI Mission & Sarvam
Sovereign AI: Exploring the IndiaAI Mission\'s goal to build indigenous computing power and datasets.
Sarvam AI: Understanding India\'s foundational models (Sarvam-105B) built for Indic languages.
Law Enforcement Use Cases: Translating regional voice notes and analyzing native-language cybercrime FIRs.
